narratives.Description: Port scripts/pr_wait_mergeable.py -- the cascade composer (#1369) that chains the Layer-3 protected-issue check AHEAD of the resilient wait loop and the merge-readiness primitive, then invokes gh pr merge, behind a single three-state exit (0 merged / 1 timeout-or-escalation / 2 config error) -- to TypeScript. It composes the merged @deftai/core pr-merge-readiness, pr-protected-issues, and pr-monitor modules and is dispatched last because it depends on them.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Create the packages/core/src/pr-wait-mergeable module porting the cascade orchestration that runs the protected-issue check first, then the wait loop and readiness primitive, then the merge invocation, composing the merged @deftai/core/pr-merge-readiness / pr-protected-issues / pr-monitor modules, with vitest unit tests in the module directory.
2. Add packages/cli/src/pr-wait-mergeable.ts exposing the TS cascade verb behind the CLI seam.
3. Add packages/cli/src/pr-wait-mergeable-parity.ts that runs the TS path and the Python scripts/pr_wait_mergeable.py oracle and asserts byte-identical output with cache off.
narratives.UserStory: As a directive maintainer, I want the wait-mergeable-and-merge cascade available in TypeScript, so that the protected-issue-first ordering and three-state cascade exit run on the TS engine with the same behavior as the Python module.

narrative.Acceptance: Running the TS cascade against a PR linking a protected issue returns the same escalation exit the Python pr_wait_mergeable.py module returns, before any merge is attempted.
Running the TS cascade returns exit 0 on merged, 1 on timeout-or-escalation, and 2 on config error, matching the Python module across the same fixtures.
Running the pr-wait-mergeable-parity binary returns exit 0 and renders byte-identical output to the Python scripts/pr_wait_mergeable.py oracle with cache off.

expected_outputs: New packages/core/src/pr-wait-mergeable module with vitest unit tests
Byte-identical parity vs scripts/pr_wait_mergeable.py under cache-off
swarm.missing_traces_justification: #1530 Wave-4b framework-internal TS port traced to GitHub issue #1730 and umbrella epic #1530; no specification.vbrief.json spec-section applies to maintainer tooling.

references.title: Issue #1730: feat(ts-migration): port pr_* monitors + cascade automation to TS (Wave 4)
